Aymen Dahmen.bio: Young goalkeeper who emerged as Tunisia's first-choice keeper after impressive performances in Ligue 1 with Montpellier. Gained valuable experience at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar and has cemented his position between the posts.
Aymen Dahmen.playingStyle: Agile, quick-reacting goalkeeper with excellent reflexes and confident handling, particularly effective at stopping shots from close range.
Aymen Dahmen.worldCup2026Role: Starting goalkeeper and Tunisia's last line of defense, bringing World Cup experience from Qatar 2022.
Aymen Dahmen.notableFact: Impressed at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar and has since established himself as Tunisia's undisputed number one.

Mohamed Dräger.bio: German-born Tunisian right-back who chose to represent Tunisia internationally. Has played across European leagues including Germany, Italy, and Switzerland with Luzern, bringing tactical versatility from his diverse experience.
Mohamed Dräger.playingStyle: Energetic, overlapping right-back who provides width in attack, delivers crosses from the right flank, and brings European tactical discipline to his defensive duties.
Mohamed Dräger.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back expected to provide attacking width and defensive solidity on Tunisia's right flank.
Mohamed Dräger.notableFact: Born and raised in Germany, he chose to represent Tunisia and has become a regular starter across multiple international tournaments.

Montassar Talbi.bio: Tall, imposing center-back who plays in France and has been a regular in Tunisia's defense across recent tournaments. A physically dominant defender who excels in aerial situations and brings size to the back line.
Montassar Talbi.playingStyle: Tall, powerful center-back who dominates in the air, makes strong challenges, and uses his physical frame to win duels against opposing strikers.
Montassar Talbi.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back and one of Tunisia's most important defenders, providing aerial security and physical presence.
Montassar Talbi.notableFact: Has represented Tunisia at the World Cup and Africa Cup of Nations, becoming a defensive mainstay for the Eagles of Carthage.

Yassine Meriah.bio: Experienced center-back and one of Tunisia's most-capped defenders with 70 international appearances. A leader at Esperance de Tunis who has captained the club in African continental competition and brings major tournament experience.
Yassine Meriah.playingStyle: Experienced, organized center-back who marshals the defensive line, communicates effectively with teammates, and makes crucial interceptions through reading the game.
Yassine Meriah.worldCup2026Role: Veteran center-back providing experience and leadership, likely as a starter or key rotation option in the heart of defense.
Yassine Meriah.notableFact: Has won the CAF Champions League with Esperance de Tunis and is one of the most decorated players in Tunisian club football.

Dylan Bronn.bio: Left-footed center-back who has spent the majority of his career in Italian football, playing in both Serie A and Serie B. A technically competent defender who contributes valuable left-footed balance to Tunisia's back line.
Dylan Bronn.playingStyle: Left-footed center-back who provides balance in defense, carries the ball forward with composure, and offers a different passing angle from the back.
Dylan Bronn.worldCup2026Role: Left-footed center-back option who provides tactical balance and distribution from the back line.
Dylan Bronn.notableFact: Scored a memorable goal at the 2018 World Cup in Russia, heading in a free kick against Panama.

Adem Zorgane.bio: Dynamic young midfielder who earned a move to Burnley in the Premier League from Belgian football. A technically gifted player who represents the future of Tunisian midfield and combines creativity with defensive contribution.
Adem Zorgane.playingStyle: Energetic, technically skilled midfielder who progresses the ball through midfield with dribbling and passing, contributes defensively, and has the quality to play in the Premier League.
Adem Zorgane.worldCup2026Role: Key young midfield starter who will be tasked with driving Tunisia's play through the middle of the park.
Adem Zorgane.notableFact: Earned a move to Burnley in the Premier League, one of the few Tunisian players competing in English top-flight football.

Youssef Msakni.bio: Tunisia's legendary forward and one of the most talented players in the nation's history, with nearly 100 caps and 25 goals. Despite playing in the Qatar Stars League, his quality, experience, and creative genius make him indispensable to the national team.
Youssef Msakni.playingStyle: Skillful, creative attacker with exceptional dribbling, an eye for spectacular goals, and the ability to produce moments of magic that change matches.
Youssef Msakni.worldCup2026Role: Veteran creative talisman and Tunisia's most dangerous attacking player, capable of producing match-winning moments of brilliance.
Youssef Msakni.notableFact: Widely regarded as one of the best Tunisian players of all time, with nearly 100 caps and 25 goals for the Eagles of Carthage.

Anis Ben Slimane.bio: Danish-born Tunisian midfielder who grew up in Denmark and developed at Brondby in the Danish Superliga. A versatile young midfielder who chose Tunisia over Denmark at international level, bringing Scandinavian tactical discipline.
Anis Ben Slimane.playingStyle: Technically clean, well-rounded midfielder who keeps the ball moving with crisp passing, provides defensive cover, and offers a goal threat from distance.
Anis Ben Slimane.worldCup2026Role: Promising young midfielder who adds depth and quality to Tunisia's central midfield options.
Anis Ben Slimane.notableFact: Born and raised in Denmark, he chose to represent Tunisia and has been tipped as one of the country's brightest midfield prospects.

Seifeddine Jaziri.bio: Clinical striker who has proven himself as a consistent goalscorer across North African and Middle Eastern leagues, including productive spells in Tunisia and Egypt with Zamalek. A natural finisher with a nose for goal.
Seifeddine Jaziri.playingStyle: Sharp, instinctive striker who makes intelligent runs in behind defenses, finishes clinically inside the box, and links well with attacking midfielders.
Seifeddine Jaziri.worldCup2026Role: Starting striker and Tunisia's primary goal threat, expected to lead the line and convert the team's chances.
Seifeddine Jaziri.notableFact: Has been a prolific scorer in North African and Middle Eastern football, consistently finding the net across multiple leagues.

Saif-Eddine Khaoui.bio: Creative attacking midfielder with experience in French football who has been a consistent contributor for Tunisia. A technically gifted player who provides invention and flair in the final third.
Saif-Eddine Khaoui.playingStyle: Clever, creative playmaker who operates between the lines, unlocks defenses with through balls, and possesses good close control in tight spaces.
Saif-Eddine Khaoui.worldCup2026Role: Creative midfield option who can play behind the striker, providing an inventive spark in attack.
Saif-Eddine Khaoui.notableFact: Developed through French football and has been a versatile attacking option for Tunisia across multiple international campaigns.

Hannibal Mejbri.bio: Highly regarded young talent who came through Manchester United's academy and is considered one of Tunisia's most exciting prospects. Named after the legendary Carthaginian general, he has the talent and fearlessness to match the name. Now at Burnley in the Premier League.
Hannibal Mejbri.playingStyle: Fiery, combative midfielder with excellent technical ability, aggressive pressing, and the courage to take on opponents in physical midfield battles.
Hannibal Mejbri.worldCup2026Role: Dynamic young midfielder expected to bring energy and intensity to Tunisia's midfield, with potential to be a breakthrough star.
Hannibal Mejbri.notableFact: Named after Hannibal Barca, the legendary Carthaginian military commander, and was the youngest Tunisian to play at a World Cup when he featured in Qatar 2022.

Mouez Hassen.bio: Experienced goalkeeper who has spent his career in French football, including Ligue 1 clubs Nice and others. A solid backup keeper with significant international experience across multiple tournaments for Tunisia.
Mouez Hassen.playingStyle: Reliable, composed goalkeeper with good positioning, strong handling, and experience in high-pressure situations at both club and international level.
Mouez Hassen.worldCup2026Role: Experienced second-choice goalkeeper providing quality backup and dressing room stability.
Mouez Hassen.notableFact: Has been part of Tunisia's squad at multiple AFCON tournaments and the World Cup, providing valuable squad continuity.

Ferjani Sassi.bio: Veteran midfielder and one of Tunisia's most experienced players with 75 caps. A set-piece specialist who has spent the latter part of his career in Qatar, maintaining his international relevance through consistent performances.
Ferjani Sassi.playingStyle: Technically gifted central midfielder with an excellent range of passing, dangerous free-kick delivery, and the ability to control the tempo of matches.
Ferjani Sassi.worldCup2026Role: Experienced midfield veteran who provides set-piece quality and calming influence in big moments.
Ferjani Sassi.notableFact: Has scored 8 goals in 75 caps including crucial goals at the Africa Cup of Nations, and is one of Tunisia's most reliable set-piece takers.

Aissa Laïdouni.bio: Energetic midfielder who burst onto the international scene with outstanding performances at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar. Originally from France, he chose Tunisia and quickly became one of the team's most important players with his tireless running.
Aissa Laïdouni.playingStyle: High-energy box-to-box midfielder with relentless pressing, incredible stamina, and the ability to cover every blade of grass while contributing to both defense and attack.
Aissa Laïdouni.worldCup2026Role: Key midfield starter whose energy and pressing will be central to Tunisia's game plan in every match.
Aissa Laïdouni.notableFact: Delivered a stunning man-of-the-match performance against France at the 2022 World Cup, helping Tunisia to a famous 1-0 victory.

Mohamed Ali Ben Romdhane.bio: Young midfielder competing in Hungary's top flight with Ferencvaros, where he has impressed in both domestic and European competition. A technically capable player who represents Tunisia's emerging generation of talent.
Mohamed Ali Ben Romdhane.playingStyle: Composed, progressive midfielder who breaks lines with his passing, carries the ball forward with confidence, and contributes goals from midfield positions.
Mohamed Ali Ben Romdhane.worldCup2026Role: Important young midfielder who provides creativity and forward thrust from central positions.
Mohamed Ali Ben Romdhane.notableFact: Has played in Europa League and Conference League matches with Ferencvaros, gaining valuable European competition experience.

Ali Abdi.bio: Experienced left-back who has spent his career in French football, becoming a reliable presence in Ligue 2 with Caen. A consistent performer with 50 international caps who provides dependable defensive cover on the left side.
Ali Abdi.playingStyle: Steady, well-rounded left-back who provides defensive solidity, makes overlapping runs when opportunities arise, and delivers accurate crosses from the left flank.
Ali Abdi.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back providing width and defensive stability on Tunisia's left flank.
Ali Abdi.notableFact: Has been a mainstay in French lower-division football and has earned 50 caps for Tunisia through consistent, reliable performances.

Ellyes Skhiri.bio: Tunisia's most valuable player and a key figure in the Bundesliga with Eintracht Frankfurt, formerly of FC Koln. A dominant defensive midfielder who has been one of the best ball-winners in German football for several seasons.
Ellyes Skhiri.playingStyle: Powerful, disciplined defensive midfielder who shields the back line, recovers possession through intelligent positioning and timing, and distributes cleanly under pressure.
Ellyes Skhiri.worldCup2026Role: Starting defensive midfielder and Tunisia's most important player, the engine that makes the team tick in the center of the park.
Ellyes Skhiri.notableFact: Has been one of the best defensive midfielders in the Bundesliga and is the highest-valued Tunisian player, worth approximately 25 million euros.

Ghaylane Chaalali.bio: Experienced domestic midfielder who has been a consistent performer for Esperance de Tunis, Tunisia's most successful club. A clever player who has contributed to multiple league titles and African continental campaigns.
Ghaylane Chaalali.playingStyle: Intelligent, tidy central midfielder who keeps the ball moving with accurate short passing, reads the game well, and provides reliable performances match after match.
Ghaylane Chaalali.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player providing midfield depth and knowledge of big-game situations from his African continental experience.
Ghaylane Chaalali.notableFact: Has won multiple Tunisian league titles and competed in the CAF Champions League with Esperance de Tunis.

Naim Sliti.bio: Experienced winger with 70 caps and 10 goals who has been a creative force for Tunisia across multiple major tournaments. A tricky, skillful player who has plied his trade in France and Saudi Arabia throughout his career.
Naim Sliti.playingStyle: Tricky, direct winger who takes on defenders with quick feet, creates chances through individual skill, and possesses a dangerous shot from outside the box.
Naim Sliti.worldCup2026Role: Experienced wide attacker who can provide creativity and directness from the wing, either starting or off the bench.
Naim Sliti.notableFact: Has represented Tunisia at both the World Cup and Africa Cup of Nations, scoring 10 goals in 70 international appearances.

Saad Bguir.bio: Versatile attacking midfielder who has been a consistent performer for Esperance de Tunis in both domestic and African continental competition. A creative player with a good eye for goal who has earned 40 international caps.
Saad Bguir.playingStyle: Creative, goal-scoring attacking midfielder who links the midfield to the attack, makes late runs into the box, and delivers decisive contributions in the final third.
Saad Bguir.worldCup2026Role: Experienced attacking option from the bench who can provide a creative spark and goal threat in the second half.
Saad Bguir.notableFact: Has been one of the most consistent performers in Tunisian club football and a key player in Esperance de Tunis' African campaigns.

Hamza Mathlouthi.bio: Veteran center-back with 25 caps who brings deep experience and professionalism to the squad. At 36, he represents the old guard of Tunisian football and provides mentorship to younger defenders.
Hamza Mathlouthi.playingStyle: Experienced, no-nonsense center-back who relies on positioning, anticipation, and game-reading ability rather than pace to defend effectively.
Hamza Mathlouthi.worldCup2026Role: Veteran defensive squad member providing leadership, dressing room influence, and emergency cover at center-back.
Hamza Mathlouthi.notableFact: One of the oldest players in Tunisia's squad, he brings a wealth of experience from years of domestic and international competition.

Farouk Ben Mustapha.bio: Long-serving veteran goalkeeper with 55 caps who has been part of Tunisia's goalkeeping rotation for over a decade. Has spent time in the Saudi Pro League with Al-Shabab, maintaining his form and fitness at the highest level.
Farouk Ben Mustapha.playingStyle: Experienced, authoritative goalkeeper who commands his area with confidence, makes decisive saves in crucial moments, and communicates well with his defense.
Farouk Ben Mustapha.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper bringing vast international experience and a calming presence in the squad.
Farouk Ben Mustapha.notableFact: Has been part of Tunisia's squad at multiple Africa Cup of Nations tournaments and World Cups, making him one of the most experienced keepers in the group.

Taha Yassine Khenissi.bio: Clinical striker who has been one of Tunisia's most reliable goalscorers with 15 goals in 40 caps. Had a prolific career in Tunisian and Middle Eastern football, known for his predatory instincts inside the penalty area.
Taha Yassine Khenissi.playingStyle: Sharp, opportunistic striker who thrives inside the penalty area, converts half-chances with clinical efficiency, and possesses good heading ability for his height.
Taha Yassine Khenissi.worldCup2026Role: Experienced backup striker providing a proven goal-scoring option from the bench.
Taha Yassine Khenissi.notableFact: Won the AFCON Golden Boot at the 2019 Africa Cup of Nations and has been one of Tunisia's most prolific strikers in recent history.

Bilel Ifa.bio: Veteran center-back with 30 caps who has spent time in Tunisian and Kuwaiti football. A dependable defensive presence who has been a consistent part of Tunisia's squad across multiple campaigns.
Bilel Ifa.playingStyle: Solid, traditional center-back who prioritizes defensive duties, makes clean tackles, and provides reliable defensive cover when called upon.
Bilel Ifa.worldCup2026Role: Experienced defensive depth option providing cover at center-back within the squad.
Bilel Ifa.notableFact: Has represented Tunisia at various levels and played club football across multiple countries in the Middle East and North Africa.

Mohamed Ben Amor.bio: Midfield stalwart at Esperance de Tunis who has been a consistent performer in Tunisian club football and earned 25 international caps. A reliable, steady presence in central midfield who keeps things ticking.
Mohamed Ben Amor.playingStyle: Disciplined, hardworking central midfielder who provides defensive screening, keeps possession with simple but effective passing, and maintains the team's shape.
Mohamed Ben Amor.worldCup2026Role: Squad midfielder providing domestic depth and the ability to fill in across central midfield positions.
Mohamed Ben Amor.notableFact: Has been a key player for Esperance de Tunis across domestic league and CAF Champions League campaigns.

Adem Zelfani.bio: Exciting young striker who represents the future of Tunisian football. Playing in France's Ligue 2 with Clermont, he has shown promising goalscoring ability at a young age and brings fresh energy to the attacking line.
Adem Zelfani.playingStyle: Quick, direct young striker who runs behind defensive lines, finishes with composure beyond his years, and creates chances through his pace and movement.
Adem Zelfani.worldCup2026Role: Young attacking prospect who provides a fresh, pacy option from the bench and represents Tunisia's future up front.
Adem Zelfani.notableFact: At just 24, he is one of the youngest players in Tunisia's squad and scored on his debut to announce himself on the international stage.
